interval_description: Deworm every 2 weeks from 2 weeks of age until 12 weeks, then monthly until 6 months of age.
notes: CAPC recommends starting early due to high prevalence of roundworms and hookworms in puppies. Use a product effective against roundworms and hookworms.

interval_description: Year-round broad-spectrum parasite control with monthly administration; fecal testing 1-2 times per year (more often for high-risk dogs).
notes: CAPC recommends year-round prevention in all regions of the US/Canada.

interval_description: Begin heartworm prevention as early as the product label allows (typically by 8 weeks of age). Administer monthly year-round.
notes: No heartworm test needed before starting in puppies under 7 months; test annually thereafter.

interval_description: Monthly heartworm preventive year-round, with annual antigen + microfilaria testing.
notes: Year-round prevention recommended even in cooler climates due to unpredictable mosquito activity.

interval_description: Year-round flea and tick prevention; product application per label (typically monthly topical/oral, or every 3 months for some isoxazolines).
notes: CAPC recommends year-round protection throughout the US/Canada.

interval_description: Deworm the dam during pregnancy and lactation per veterinarian guidance to reduce transmission to puppies.
notes: Specific product and timing should be directed by a veterinarian; CAPC notes perinatal transmission of roundworms and hookworms is common.

interval_description: Deworm every 2 weeks from 3 weeks of age through 9 weeks, then monthly until 6 months.
notes: Focus on roundworms and hookworms; use products approved for kittens at appropriate ages.

interval_description: Year-round broad-spectrum parasite control with monthly administration; annual fecal testing (twice yearly for outdoor/hunting cats).
notes: Indoor-only cats still benefit from year-round prevention due to indirect exposure.

interval_description: Monthly feline heartworm preventive year-round. No approved adulticide exists for cats, so prevention is critical.
notes: Heartworm disease in cats is often underdiagnosed; prevention recommended for all cats including indoor.

interval_description: Year-round flea prevention with feline-labeled product (typically monthly). Tick prevention where exposure risk exists.
notes: Never apply canine permethrin products to cats — toxic to felines.

interval_description: Treat when tapeworm segments or fleas are observed; year-round flea control prevents most Dipylidium tapeworm transmission.
notes: Outdoor hunting cats should be screened more frequently.
